scenario: Stellar Logistics is onboarding 12 new operators to Camunda 8.8 Operate for a fleet that processes 9000 shipments per day. Before the operators can practice basic Operate navigation on the sample order-process model, the team lead wants to confirm the documented prerequisites that the userguide states up front.
question: Which prerequisites does the basic Operate navigation section assume have already been completed?

options.text: A BPMN process has been deployed to Zeebe and at least 1 process instance has been created on the same cluster, so the operator has something to inspect in Operate
A BPMN process has been deployed to a Camunda 7 engine and at least 1 process instance has been created on that engine before any Operate navigation is possible from the dashboard
A DMN decision has been deployed to Zeebe and at least 1 evaluated decision instance exists, so Operate can render the dashboard cards needed for navigation
A Web Modeler project has been created and shared with at least 5 collaborators, so Operate can show the deployed processes by name and version on the dashboard

a.text: Correct. The basic-operate-navigation page opens with a note stating that the section and the following one assume you have deployed a process to Zeebe and created at least one process instance; otherwise the dashboard panels and instance views have nothing to display.

b.text: Incorrect. Camunda 8 Operate reads state from the Zeebe broker on the cluster; the prerequisite cited by the docs is a deployment to Zeebe, not to a Camunda 7 engine.

c.text: Incorrect. The prerequisite called out in the note is a deployed process and a running process instance; DMN decisions and decision instances are not what this section assumes.

d.text: Incorrect. Web Modeler collaborators are not mentioned as a prerequisite for Operate navigation; the requirement stated in the note is a deployed process plus at least one running instance.

explanation: The introduction to basic Operate navigation explicitly tells readers it assumes they have deployed a process to Zeebe and created at least one process instance, and points unfamiliar readers to the guides section. The same order-process.bpmn model is then reused throughout the subsequent navigation steps.
